content:
sorry for typing so much, I got more emotional than I thought I would 

1- 1st of December, 2023. Honestly what brought me here is an odd story. A Lady I know gave me an account, and told me to play for her while translating things for her convenience.

2- Is it weird to say the very beginning? Carving my name onto a gravestone, the moment it happened, I was like “Ah. This is something special”. It’s something hard to explain. I’ve personally carved peoples names into gravestones before, some of those graves sit so far in the wilderness that they will never be found, yet I carved them anyway. Some of them I knew would rot away, carved crudely onto something no respectable person would call a gravestone, yet I did it anyways. Maybe it’s just personal, but carving my own name onto a gravestone resonated with me, because it was an action I know that I’d never do for myself.

3- I have no preference. Like watching a bright star in the night sky, simply observing it, regardless of what it does, is my greatest joy. 

4- no comments on this one.

5- Thank you so very much for everything you have all done. I’ll be honest, by mid 2024, I was worried. I was worried that this game, that burned so brightly, would be snuffed out in front of my eyes. I was worried that the ember that I saw that had the potential to become a wildfire would be snuffed out before it could grow. I’ll be honest, I dont log in every day. I’m not well connected to the community or the culture, I only joined this server to leave this message, and I think the last time I pulled on the gacha was half a year ago. Equally, this game is truly special for me, as a game sure, but as a piece of art. For a video game to capture as much of my heart as literature, to so thoroughly enrapture me in ways I have not felt since my first time reading the Iliad, is truly something I cannot put into words, so I hope that this admittedly overly long spiel will get across at least a fragment of my gratitude.

**5. Is there anything you want to say to our team?**

Thank you. Truly.

Thank you for keeping this project alive. Thank you for not giving up on it. Thank you for continuing to create something that moves people so deeply.

To the artists, your work is breathtaking.
To the writers, thank you for making me cry, hope, and love.
To the developers and everyone whose work we do not always see, you are the foundation of this world.

And the music… the music touches something very deep inside me. I adore the radio dramas. I have listened to them over and over again. The archive feature is precious to me because I actually go back and relive those moments.

I am also incredibly grateful that you are open with the community. That you listen, respond, and react. That kind of openness is rare and it means more than you might realize.

Thank you for allowing queer representation in the game. As a member of the community, that matters to me deeply. Playing as Leonora and seeing her interactions with women fills me with warmth and joy. It feels sincere. It feels natural. It makes me feel seen.

And please consider more merch with shipping to Europe. There are players here who would love to support you even more.

Morimens is not just a game to me.
It is a world I care about with my whole heart.
Thank you for creating it. ❤️ 


I’m sorry for the long message and for sending it in parts. It simply didn’t fit into one, and it felt important to say all of this.

Thank you for giving Morimens another chance—and welcome back since Pollux! We’re really glad the story, translation, and gameplay clicked for you this time.

<:realm_chaos:1437283650809888768> <:realm_aequor:1437283645051375777> <:realm_caro:1437283648134058004> <:realm_ultra:1437283545784651926> 
As for Realm Mastery, it’s something we’ve wanted to rework for a long time. However, we also want to minimize disruption to everyone’s builds and progress, so we’ll likely move toward it gradually in a way that causes the least impact.

We’d love to share more behind-the-scenes dev stories with you in the future. Thank you for your support!

1. I started playing around July 2025 when I first saw it on the app store and on the Steam page. I'm fond of the mystery-horror aspects (Lovecraftian stuff is always so fun) and seeing Morimens gotten me intrigued and curious. I enjoy looking through all the character designs and got hooked by Goliath (laughs). I saw him and declare that I will make him the strongest as I can in my mind. <:goliath_spend:1468259820875415728> 

2. There's multiple scenes that touched me since I'm quite an emotional and soft-hearted person. It makes me want to cherish the awakeners more and other characters too. 

3. I would like more affinity type of stories (that are fluffy or more characterization). For example, I just want to see what it is like just hanging out with one of the awakeners. Or more messages since I like to see more interactions and fun conversations. It feels lonely without seeing an awakener's message from the communicator after all the available messages are used up 🙏
Also, I would love to see more costumes and characters too! I do enjoy the designs a lot and curious of the flavor text for the costumes. Plus, I want to see how the main story will continue to develop as well!

4. I enjoy the behind-the-scenes stories very much! (It does intensify my love for Goliath and Aequor fellas) I want to know everyone's sides and more of their own personal development! I also would love to know about the Mythag's students and teachers too. It would be nice to see and learn about them more. Also, I would like to see Caecus' since it would be nice to see how he came to be. <:Caecus_prayer:1459077248089853962> 

5. Thank you so much for making this game! Learning about how the game came to be, its struggles, and how you guys overcome those hardships truly touch my heart. It made me want to support the game so strongly that I wish to see what more to come! Thank you for staying so strong!! May you never lose your path and be forever loved!!

1、暑假最后几天（2025.7.29），无聊去翻Steam商城，看到了《忘却前夜》。最开始是因为玩法入坑的，后来才发现其他部分也不错。

2、之前提到过，是忘却篇三、四章。我对故事没有太多要求，只要：内容完整、故事线清晰（可以在读完复盘时才清晰）、情节逻辑自洽、有真情实感，就算的上合格了。这两章，要求基本满足，而且故事设定新颖、几个冲突贯穿整个故事，情节很吸引我、人物形象塑造得立体、主旨鲜明且震撼到我了。

3、我目前对玩法没什么要求，主要希望你们能把故事写好。

4、喜欢。一些制作方面的、设计方面的、以及背景方面的。

5、对剧情、角色方面：
         “徐”的设计着实让我眼前一亮，因为我看到了一种结合中国风的尝试，而这个尝试很成功。在现在这个时代，各种二游满天飞，故事并不缺乏，那么怎样才能脱颖而出？
         我认为，就四个字：守正创新。
         守正，首先是要有一个底蕴在，要扎根于博大精深的文化上（不论是克苏鲁神话还是中华优秀传统文化）。从现实的角度，这样的故事本土玩家容易买单，因为这讲的就是他们自己的历史、他们自己的故事；从创作的角度，这能给你们提供源源不断的灵感。其次是要源于现实。当下，我们一方面在寻找好故事，一方面在寻找我们自己。好故事，是能让读者共鸣的，这就要求作者写出“接地气”的作品。《忘却前夜》制作组的故事，就是一个非常独特的经历，这也是其他游戏所没有的，我希望你们能借此创作出更精彩的内容！
         创新，是要基于你们游戏的现实情况，为发展注入活力。如何把中国风带进爱手艺的时间观中？如何将爱手艺的设定发展得更贴合当下需要？如何在原有的故事体系中开创新的篇章？这些是你们必须要面对的问题。我个人是十分喜爱中华文化的，也希望你们能进行多文化的融合，以开放包容的姿态迎接未来。最重要的，是要有自己的想法。
         创新很重要，但我希望你们能把一些漏洞先补了。按信中：星辰篇是意料之外的，那么有漏洞可以理解。另外信中提到在修复遗留问题，其实新问题也是要修的。目前，最明显的“星辰正位之刻”这个标题都意义不明，空有一个壳。星辰正位在哪？为什么这个时刻那么特殊？

对策划：
         你们选择了这样一个竞争激烈的赛道，也在最困难的时候坚持下来了，这让我想起了本人的高三。
         废话少说，总体上，你们做的很好。这里我只提一点个人的看法。
         作为游戏策划，你们需要清楚地认识到游戏当下最需要什么、最突出的优点是什么、未来如何发展。面面俱到几乎是不可能的，因此要学会扬长避短。依我之见，《忘却前夜》的文化方面做的是比玩法方面要好的。玩法依旧是一个游戏的核心，这是不可动摇的，但从当下的情况看，可以考虑加点力度去打造一个《忘却前夜》自己的文化生态圈。对于游戏而言，这是一个非常重要的宣传方式，也是让玩家长久留存的引力。最主要的，就是鼓励二创，与玩家多多交流。
         说实话，看多了玩家批评你们的言论，我都有点同情了。但是，他们所指出的问题确实是客观存在的，是需要被解决的。希望你们能虚心纳下、慎始敬终，在听取玩家们的反馈时，也要有自己的判断。请记住：游戏的命运，很大一部分掌握在你们手里。
         玩法上，你们同样要学会守正创新。从信中的内容来看，我相信你们已经这样做了。
         最后，我希望全体策划都能花时间去读（或再读一遍）屈原的《渔父》。

对B.I.A.V全体人员：
         你们创造的世界是我第一个投入如此之大的世界，也收到了你们的反馈。继续努力，心态乐观一点，好好活下去！我会继续在二创圈子努力！（即使我可能没在你们最需要社区力量的时候入坑）
         借《黑神话•悟空》里黄风大王的一句台词，送给你们所有人：
         我来助你！

1. November 2024 was technically the first time I played, but I would realistically place to late into 2.3.4's patch, just before the 2nd Anniversary update began. I don't remember the exact day. My interest was piqued by the art, the music, and the fact it was full of references to the Mythos, which I dearly love the works of.

2. Chapter 1 and 2's endings were among the first that hooked me originally, but the Act 1 finale was easily one of the most touching scenes, especially with the voices for Ramona and Leonora to go with it.

3.  More Traphase ability to play against bots, permanent roguelike game modes not unlike the Train... 

4. I love hearing about what goes on behind the curtain, as the glimpse into what inspired various characters and decisions in the game's design is rather interesting. I do wonder what went into settling on Ramona as a core character and as the mascot of the game.

5. Thank you for pushing on!

1. 27/11/2024. I came across the game while scrolling through the free section of the steam store. At the time I had spent a lot of time playing Slay the Spire so when I saw that there was a StS-like game that had Lovecraftian elements in themes and character designs AND that contains gambling, I was quickly captivated. 

2. I haven't really gone through the story as I was put off by the machine translations at first, and then had to focus on other aspects of life after the proper localisations. Although I couldn't answer this question properly as of now, I will be going back to experience the story when I am able to.

3. I am quite interested in how the gameplay will be reinvented after Astral Reign. I just can't really imagine how else it can be changed.

4. I do find the behind the scenes stories to be interesting, gives the characters more depth and allows us players to get a grasp of how the world goes about. On that note, I won't say no to even more Ghelot content.

5. When I first started Morimens, I had no clue on the events that was happening behind the scenes with development, so my first impressions of the game is through the uniqueness of it's teambuilding and gameplay. After a while, I found the game more enjoyable each time I played, and eventually began to frequent the game before bed to take my mind off of whatever had happened on that day. I played the game through some pretty stressful times in my life and found the game to be exactly what I wanted to see from the Gacha gaming space with it's horror-esque elements and rouge-like gameplay, added onto the freedom I felt while building a team of characters. I am very grateful for this game to exist, for something so unique to be alive in this market saturated with so much games with similar elements. I'm soon starting college, and I'm very grateful to have this game accompany me as I move into a new phase of my life. Thank you B.I.A.V, truly.
噢 可以給二創的前幾名多一點特別的實體周邊獎勵 我覺得這個不錯

1. i started playing morimens on the recruitment campaign for my friend who wanted the doll skin. i didn't intend to stay, but i got hooked by the gameplay and the story and couldn't put the game down. also clementine's design ❤️

2. i cried at aigis and jenkin, and also was sobbing at the finale of arc 1. ex oblivione is such a beautiful song and having voice acting for keeper... it was so bittersweet.

3. i'm a big fan of roguelikes and hope to see more strategy and variety in a large roguelike mode. the hectic skybound rail is Okay to me but it still feels very similar to the normal gameplay loop. i also would love to see exploration on stages make a comeback as i really liked the maps! but primarily i would love to see more story which is where morimens truly shines.

4. i love hearing behind the scenes! i don't know much about lovecraft so i would love to hear about how characters were inspired by lovecraftian lore. especially keeper, or the lore behind the realms!

5. thank you for all your hard work, openness and determination. it means a lot to me to have such open communication with a dev team, when most gacha are produced by companies that don't care about players (especially your attention to the global playerbase). morimens is such a unique game with story and art that doesn't just exist to follow a trend and that's really special. it's an honour to support you all and i hope the game can continue onwards for more years to come ❤️

1. It was Ryker. I saw his design floating online and it instanty caught my eye. A detetive wolfman who fights by throwing dice is pretty amazing. Especially loved the use of gold in his design. Signed up and just managed to snag him before his first event ended.

2. There are a lot. Jenkin's last heist, Lily and her mom, Murphy's self-sacrifice, Clementine's loss of humanity, everything in the end of Arc 1. Also the beach episode and fighting cats.

3. I'm intrested in the small stories. The lives of ordinary students, staff, and citizens, the Keeper's daily routine, the layout of Londinium and other towns, what the Awakeners are doing on their free time. Sorel's and Salvador's sidestories introduced new locations and Uvhash and Agrripa's Fridge Raid was funny and explored more of these characters and life at the School. Also another furry character. Mh'ithrha is available~

4. Yes! I would love to hear more of your process for character design. The research to make them accurate with the lore, the inspiration and thoughts in crafting them, the underlying concepts behind the character. Also would be interested in hearing more of your Call of Chuthulu sessions. Those always have good stories in them and it's neat how one inspired the creation of Xu.

5. You made something really special here. Not just in the game, but also in reality. You made choices, you had hardships, you both met and said goodbye to many people, yet you pushed through. Your efforts have meaning, and is now resonating and reflected in both many people worldwide and in those standing with you. You did good. All of you.

content:
1 - around 2 or 3 months ? Kath design made me start playing 

2 - the last scene in arc 1 

3 - CORPOSANT CONTENT AND APPEARANCE ON MAIN STORY <:Corpo_advance:1459080545882341376> 

4 - MORE CORPOSANT AND AURITA . 

5 - thank you for your efforts , 🫠 .

1. 你是在什么时候，因为什么原因开始玩「忘却前夜」的？
图鲁卡池期间，看有朋友在玩感觉挺有意思就入坑了

2. 最打动你的，是游戏中的哪一幕？请与我们分享一下！
一个是在忘却篇主线结束后 来自遗忘 那首歌开始播放的时候，守密人那种直面明知无法战胜的存在仍冲上前去和遗书里写的曾经的样子的变化，让人想起整个主线剧情，真的很感动
还有就是一周年时跨越维度的来信，在二游里见过的最有创意也最有意义的活动！给人一种“我不是孤身一人”的感觉，无论是剧情里还是游戏外。期待以后还会有

3. 你最希望看到怎样的内容更新？为什么？
肉鸽把大直线改改吧我求你了🥺让我选路吧走格子也行
神图！神图快点端上来吧，给看一眼立绘也行啊

4. 你喜欢徐的幕后故事吗？你还想要看到哪些类型的幕后故事？
喜欢！希望能看到星辰篇故事和角色的创作历程

5. 有什么想要对我们团队成员说的话吗？我会记录下来在翻译为团队的母语后告诉团队成员
忘却前夜可以说是我玩过的最好玩的二游了，一定要活下去啊你死了我们吃什么😭
对小猫：忘却前夜整个游戏的玩法很优秀，很感谢你的坚持能让我们玩上了它。与此同时，纵观从开服到现在，我觉得有些时候你有必要把你的“小巧思”稍微收一收，我能明白你出于想让忘却前夜变得更好，但有些时候你的“巧思”反而适得其反（最典型的比如福袋削弱、羊妈周边、星辰篇开门时的各种debuff）。忘却前夜能做到今天玩家们的功劳不必多说，你要知道现在能和你提意见甚至批评的玩家绝对都是希望游戏做的更好的，希望你能更加重视玩家们的意见，以其为今后的主要修改的方向而不是把玩家们的意见仅作为参考，想改就改不想改就放着不管了（二周年版本做的一系列调整就很好，期待未来做出更多更好的调整）。
最后提点建议。
1 你游产能不足这是众所周知的，但我不希望“产能不足”四个字成为挡箭牌以此不做调整。问题可以改的慢但是不能放任不管，哪怕每个版本都只有一点小的调整都好。从2.0星辰篇开始不管是主线还是支线剧情明显大不如前，有些甚至可以说纯凑数了。
2 活动从莉莉开始到现在为止模式都一个样，以前莉莉、神父、24、米粒等还有自己的选关界面和完整的活动机制这些，现在每期ui都完全一样就贴个背景了，活动做的可以说很敷衍了。未来可以至少把2.0pv里角色的活动做的更好更认真一些吗，给组独立的共鸣都是好的。
3 最新的肉鸽想法是好的，但是大直线多少有点无聊了，随便做个选路（都说了致敬尖塔了那你倒是用啊！）哪怕用以前比较大的走格子地图我觉得也比大直线好。肉鸽玩法做好了是很能增加在线时长的，希望多做优化，争取做出明日方舟肉鸽的效果（
但是无论如何忘却前夜都是一款非常优秀的游戏！它带给我了很大的震撼与感动，玩的很爽，福利很不错，很多系统机制（比如角色和武器的养成，抽卡，装备）我觉得甚至值得很多市面上的大二游去学。美术真的强！角色们都非常美丽！祝忘却前夜越来越好！祝制作组的大家新年快乐！

1. I started playing during the Mouchette event. I know about Morimens from a meme videos on X that came across my timeline and got interested~

2. Aigis' scene in the clock tower 😭</3

3. The background music! And also the new costumes (they're all so beautiful).

4. Yes! Maybe from the team that created the background music? I'd love to hear a little bit about what went into creating the music for the story.

5. Thank you for this amazing game! Love it!<333
列车别的我都不说的好玩还是好玩的，但是要拿完奖励需要至少十多把困难以上而且还都需要不同配队去打，这是否有点，看到三层那个几百万血的帷幕我头都大了，我过年啥都不干天天缩在家里打忘却前夜吗<:Agrippa_Stare:1468259401189294150>

1. When did you start playing Morimens, and what brought you here?
-My Turn
2. Which scene in the game touched you the most?
-Unfortunately I skips stories 😔, the effects of the bosses are very cool though
3. What kind of content updates are you most excited to see?
-Characters and QOL updates that make the game feel more rewarding, and balance changes that make all characters viable in main game without having to investing everything we have.
4. Do you enjoy these behind-the-scenes stories? Who else would you like to hear about?
-Great story, Unfortunately I do not know about other devs, but it has been a long and interesting journey since My Turn.
5. Is there anything you want to say to our team? (I will translate your messages and share them with the staﬀ!)
-Great job, your game is a great creation that has been enjoyed by many, hope you guys continue to develop it so more people can enjoy it more!!

I stumbled upon Morimens in Qapp by accident and was surprised that I hadn't known about such a beautiful project. One glance at Doll was enough for me to give it a try. I knew that no other game would have such stunning characters—I really appreciate this art style. The way the artists blend the inanimate with the living, create dark moods, skillfully work with colors and accents, and deliver light fanservice without slipping into generic porn gacha characters is simply magnificent, even despite some anatomical issues.
Even nearly naked Helot doesn't look vulgar thanks to the artists' skillful work 
(and, for example, Murphy: Fauxborn looks like a cheap slut because of her outfit—she'd benefit from a longer skirt. That was a bit disappointing). When I first downloaded the game, I spent the first 2 hours just admiring the characters. I've never had a game where over 60% of the characters made me want to pull for them. (I'd love to see an alternate skin and voice for Agrippa; she looks like a modern alt girl. Also skins for Pickman, Murphy: Fauxborn, and Pandia to make them look calmer and more moderate.)
I don't like gacha games and would have uninstalled Morimens long ago if I didn't know it was made by actual game developers, not gacha grinders. I quickly got hooked on the gameplay, and it was hard to pull me away from the game content. (I hate the tiles—they're so clunky and completely identical throughout the entire first arc. It would be cool to completely rework them, like in other games of similar genres. So that there's real choice in them, some variability in gameplay, rather than just clearing the entire area. I also want to thank those who implemented the Jenkins level and the Kum hunt moment in Lily's storyline.)

The characters' abilities are brilliantly designed—combining and studying them was incredibly engaging, and even now, after 600+ hours, I realize there's still so much to learn. Almost every character here can play their role and clear content; even the starter characters can handle everything with proper understanding
(it would be cool if regular Helot could function as a DPS in the current meta, not just a support for Mouchette).

Beyond the standard gameplay, Morimens has another game mode—PvP—where the number of character copies and external spending don't affect your team's strength. Thank you for this fairness.

My favorite moment was probably from Chapter 6, when the false god threw Murphy from a height onto the deck:

All her bones shattered on impact, her ribcage torn open by a massive wound, then shattered into pieces by the false god's strike.

Waves relentlessly crashed onto the deck, washing away one pool of blood after another beneath it, as if striving to completely erase all traces of her presence.

(Please release a one-star Murphy skin in the jacket from the story!)

And all the gory scenes, descriptions of the cruel pain endured by the characters—I love all of it. Caro realm is my favorite, and I'd love to see much more cruelty than there is now. It's a shame you had to cut content due to local restrictions (I saw old upgrade material sprites as embryos—they looked hilarious). The plots of Chapters 7, 8, and 9, as well as 1-2, aren't really my thing yet, but 2-2 is cool. The same goes for character development—they lack depth and humanity; they're all too simplistic.

Take Nautila: in her messages, her memory issues are played for comedy, like "haha, she forgot who she was texting, forgot what she wanted, and other silly stuff." In a game centered on memory themes, you expect deeper handling of a character with such a problem. Real-life examples of people suffering from similar ailments are horrific—why not show that horror and pain in full? Forgetting loved ones and family, forgetting yourself, your life. Then rebuilding with help from loved ones, under new rules—like writing notes, keeping a diary, making maps, etc. And on that foundation, you could build a cute comedic element that would always carry a bittersweet sadness, because some wounds don't heal.
The same with Winkle: in messages, she's on the verge of tears, breaking down, fear consuming her, desperately needing help—and the protagonist responds like "calm down, idiot, turn on the light and stop bothering me." It's unbearable.

And Aurita's intelligence? She's literally like a dog... why?

What about Agrippa and Uvhash's personalities? Just because they're from the past doesn't mean they have to be mental primitives.

Overall, the game has plenty of weak spots as well as strong ones. I love it for its uniqueness, its drive to improve, and its fight.

I enjoyed these dev notes, and it'd be really interesting to hear how you negotiate collaborations with other developers.

I'd like to give special thanks to the artists who drew Sorel, the writers who scripted Ramona and Murphy, Ai Yamamoto, for the voices of her characters—it's only because of her voice acting that I pulled for Thais, and all the developers who implemented draft mode in PvP—you're the best.
